title: The Top 10 Herbal Teas for Managing Psoriasis Flare-Ups
intro_desc: Herbal teas for psoriasis are natural remedies that help alleviate symptoms of this chronic skin condition by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and soothing discomfort. The best herbal teas for psoriasis include Urtica dioica, Silybum marianum, Taraxacum officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Equisetum arvense, Calendula officinalis, Aloe vera, Camellia sinensis, Cinchona officinalis, and Ginkgo biloba. If left untreated, psoriasis can significantly impact daily life, causing emotional distress, social isolation, and limited mobility, ultimately affecting one's quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tea, also known as stinging nettle tea, has been used for centuries to help manage psoriasis symptoms. The leaves of the stinging nettle plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis. The antioxidants in Urtica dioica tea may also help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of psoriasis lesions. Additionally, the tea has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, meaning it can help regulate the body's immune response, which is often overactive in people with psoriasis. Some studies suggest that Urtica dioica tea may help reduce the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body, which can contribute to the severity of psoriasis symptoms. Drinking Urtica dioica tea may also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Urtica dioica tea make it a potential natural remedy for managing psoriasis symptoms.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for psoriasis because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making it a natural and effective treatment option for people with this condition.

Tannins are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, and also have antioxidant effects that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

Kaempferol is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing and calming psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal teas for psoriasis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with psoriasis, start by gathering dried Melissa leaves, which are also known as lemon balm. You can purchase them from a health food store or grow them in your garden. Begin by boiling water in a kettle or pot, and then let it cool for a few minutes to prevent the leaves from becoming bitter. Next, add one teaspoon of dried Melissa leaves to a cup and pour the cooled water over them. Allow the leaves to steep for 5-7 minutes to release their medicinal properties. Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to use them in moderation. Drinking this tea may help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with psoriasis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tea, made from the gel of the aloe vera plant, is a natural remedy that can help soothe psoriasis symptoms. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy patches on the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe barbadensis tea can reduce redness and swelling of the skin. The tea also has soothing and moisturizing properties that can help calm itchy skin and lock in moisture, reducing the risk of dry, cracked skin. Aloe vera contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other antioxidants that help protect the skin from damage and promote healthy skin cell growth. Drinking aloe barbadensis tea may also help reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger psoriasis flare-ups.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce redness and itching of psoriasis by blocking the release of certain chemicals that cause inflammation in the skin.
Aloin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help to reduce redness, itching, and scaling associated with the condition.

Flavonoids, also known as lemon balm, are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling in the skin, thereby providing relief from psoriasis symptoms.

Polysaccharides, also known as lemon balm, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriasis, thereby promoting healthy skin and alleviating symptoms of the condition.
Aloe-emodin may help with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling, and also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage.

remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for psoriasis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that can help with psoriasis, start by gathering dried lemon balm leaves or fresh leaves that have been dried properly. If you're using fresh leaves, simply chop them into small pieces and let them sit for a few hours to let the excess moisture evaporate. Next, boil one quart of water in a large pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add one tablespoon of dried lemon balm leaves or two tablespoons of fresh leaves to the pot, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea infuser or a piece of cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add honey to the tea if you prefer it sweet, but be sure to consult with a doctor before consuming it if you have any medical conditions. Drinking one to two cups of Melissa officinalis tea per day may help soothe the symptoms of psoriasis, including inflammation and itching. However, it's essential to note that while herbal teas can be beneficial, they should not replace medical treatment. Consult with a doctor before using Melissa officinalis tea as a treatment for psoriasis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum, also known as milk thistle, is a plant that has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The seeds of the plant contain a powerful compound called silymarin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. When made into a tea, silymarin can help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis. The anti-inflammatory properties can also help slow down the growth of skin cells, which is a common issue in psoriasis patients. Additionally, silymarin has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Drinking Silybum marianum tea may also help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the itching and scaling associated with psoriasis. Some studies suggest that silymarin can also help reduce the production of cytokines, which are proteins that can trigger inflammation in the body.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Melissa officinalis herbal tea that helps with psoriasis, first you need to dry some Melissa officinalis leaves. You can either air-dry them by tying the branches together and hanging them upside down, or use a dehydrator on a low heat setting. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can store them in an airtight container for later use. To make the tea, pour a cup of boiling water over a tablespoon of the dried leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. You can add honey to taste, but it's best to consume the tea without any additives. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, which can help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.

supplementary_best: For psoriasis relief, a combination of herbal teas from 'Aloe barbadensis', 'Calendula officinalis', and 'Equisetum arvense' may be beneficial. 'Aloe barbadensis' or aloe vera, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and calm the skin. It contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which can reduce redness and irritation.  'Calendula officinalis' or pot marigold, has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including psoriasis. Its flowers are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.  'Equisetum arvense', also known as horsetail, is a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. It contains silica, a mineral that can help strengthen the skin and reduce inflammation. The combination of these three herbal teas can work together to provide relief from the symptoms of psoriasis, such as redness, itching, and inflammation.
supplementary_avoid: If you have psoriasis, there are some herbal teas you should avoid or use with caution. Chamomile tea, for example, can be a problem because it can make the skin more sensitive. Ginger tea may also not be suitable because it can cause skin irritation and increase inflammation. Licorice root tea can be a concern because it can raise blood pressure and worsen psoriasis symptoms.  Sage tea can also be a problem for people with psoriasis because it can cause skin dryness and irritation. Fennel tea may not be suitable due to its potential to cause skin irritation and increase inflammation. Dandelion root tea can be a concern because it can cause skin dryness and irritation.  Turmeric tea can be a problem for people with psoriasis because it can cause skin irritation and increase inflammation. Red clover tea can be a concern because it can raise blood pressure and worsen psoriasis symptoms.  These herbal teas may interact with medications or worsen psoriasis symptoms, so it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming them.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bathing in Dead Sea salt water can help with psoriasis. The high concentration of minerals like magnesium and potassium in the water can help reduce inflammation and soothe dry skin. Magnesium, in particular, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help calm the skin and reduce redness. Dead Sea salt water baths can also help improve blood circulation, which can help deliver oxygen and nutrients to the skin, promoting healing and reducing symptoms.  Olive oil can also be beneficial for psoriasis. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties can help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. Applying olive oil to affected areas can also help reduce the formation of thick, scaly plaques, which are a common symptom of psoriasis.  Aloe vera gel has been used for centuries to soothe and calm irritated skin, including skin affected by psoriasis. Its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties can help lock in moisture and soothe dry skin. Aloe vera gel can also help reduce the formation of thick, scaly plaques, and promote healing and regeneration of skin cells.  Another natural remedy that can help with psoriasis is vitamin D. Vitamin D is essential for maintaining healthy skin, and people with psoriasis often have low levels of vitamin D. Taking vitamin D supplements or exposing yourself to sunlight can help increase vitamin D levels, which can help reduce symptoms of psoriasis. Vitamin D also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling.  Some people with psoriasis find that eating a diet rich in omega-3 fatty acids, which are found in fatty fish, can help reduce symptoms. Omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling, while also promoting healing and regeneration of skin cells. Omega-3 fatty acids can also help improve blood circulation, which can help deliver oxygen and nutrients to the skin.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with psoriasis often experience other health issues, including joint pain and stiffness due to psoriatic arthritis. Aloe vera gel can help with this by reducing inflammation and soothing the joints. Turmeric, with its active compound curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can ease joint pain and swelling.  Those with psoriasis are also at a higher risk of developing heart disease and high blood pressure. Ginger has been shown to help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation. Additionally, it can reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heart disease.  Some people with psoriasis may experience anxiety and depression due to the emotional impact of the condition. St. John's Wort, a herbal remedy, has been traditionally used to treat mild depression and anxiety. Its active compounds, hyperforin and hypericin, can help regulate mood and reduce symptoms of depression.  Psoriasis patients may also experience digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), due to inflammation and immune system activation. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the gut. It can also help regulate bowel movements and reduce symptoms of IBS.  Ginkgo biloba may also be beneficial for those with psoriasis, as it can help improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This can be especially helpful for people with psoriatic arthritis, as it can help reduce joint pain and swelling.
